hypothesis: When one modality is systematically absent or corrupted at inference time (e.g., XRD patterns are unavailable for amorphous or nanostructured materials), the cross-modal predictive training objective of X-MoPA will enable the model to hallucinate a latent representation of the missing modality from the remaining two with sufficient fidelity that downstream property prediction accuracy degrades by no more than 10% relative to the full three-modality case, because the latent predictors have explicitly learned to encode the mutual information shared across modalities during pretraining—and this robustness will be measurably greater than that of concatenation-based multimodal baselines trained without cross-modal prediction objectives.
